# Blackspot

> Blackspot is on-device video quality-control (QC) software for macOS. It scans a finished video export and reports the exact timestamps of defects, so an editor checks a handful of marks instead of re-watching the whole runtime.

Blackspot runs entirely on your Mac. Your footage never leaves the machine; nothing is uploaded.

## What it is
- Product: Blackspot — Video QC for Mac
- Platform: macOS 11 or later, Apple Silicon
- Site: https://tryblackspot.com
- Download: https://github.com/myrondoesnotcode/blackspot/releases/latest/download/Blackspot.dmg
- Privacy: 100% on-device and offline. No upload, no account.

## What it detects
- Black frames — single black frames and longer black segments
- Frozen / stuck frames
- Audio dropouts — dead air and silence
- Audio spikes / clipping
- Color glitches — green/magenta/blue render corruption
- Exposure — over-exposed / washed-out sections
- Flash / short shots — stray sub-second clips between cuts
- On-screen text spelling — misspelled titles, lower-thirds and captions, read on-device
- Skipped / dropped frames
- Corrupt / decode-error frames

## How it works
Blackspot analyzes the export and returns each defect with an exact timestamp. A built-in player jumps straight to each flagged moment for confirmation. Flags are candidates, not verdicts — the editor confirms each one in the player.

## Premiere Pro
Blackspot also ships a Premiere Pro panel that finds timeline gaps and defects and drops a clickable marker on the sequence, without exporting the video or leaving Premiere.

## Who it's for
Video editors and producers working with long-form content — podcasts, interviews, and talks — who need to QC a full export before delivery.
